objective_definitions.lateness_minutes: Sum, over occurrences in final_schedule whose scheduled start is later than their original start_local (in the event's own timezone), of (scheduled_start_utc - original_start_utc) in minutes. Occurrences kept at or moved earlier than their original start contribute 0.
objective_definitions.moved_count: Number of occurrences in final_schedule whose scheduled start_local/venue differs from their original start_local/venue.
objective_definitions.priority_score: Sum of the priority field of every occurrence present in final_schedule.
objective_definitions.travel_minutes: Sum over every pair of occurrences that are chronologically adjacent in final_schedule (no other scheduled occurrence starts between them) of the travel_matrix.csv minutes between their two venues. Adjacent occurrences at the same venue contribute 0.

reply_rule_definitions.conflict_with_alternate: Else if conflict=true and alternate_available=true, its category is PROPOSE_ALTERNATE.
reply_rule_definitions.missing_required_details: If a message's missing_required_details=true, its category is REQUEST_INFO, regardless of any other field.
reply_rule_definitions.no_legal_alternative: Else if no_legal_alternative=true, its category is DECLINE.
reply_rule_definitions.requires_escalation: Else if requires_escalation=true, its category is ESCALATE.

scheduling_rules.after_hours_rule: An occurrence whose start_local time-of-day (in its own timezone) is at or after after_hours_cutoff_local is only allowed in final_schedule if after_hours_ok=true or vip_override=true; otherwise that start_local/venue combination is not a legal placement for the occurrence.
scheduling_rules.conflict_rule: Two scheduled occurrences may never overlap in time. Two scheduled occurrences at different venues that do not overlap still conflict if the gap between them is smaller than the travel_matrix.csv minutes required between their venues; occurrences at the same venue never require a travel gap.
scheduling_rules.dependency_rule: If occurrence B lists occurrence A's event_id in its dependencies, and both A and B appear in final_schedule, B's scheduled start must be no earlier than A's scheduled end. If A is not in final_schedule, B has no ordering constraint from this rule.
scheduling_rules.movable_rule: An occurrence with movable=false has exactly one legal placement (its original start_local/venue) and can only be included as-is or left out of final_schedule entirely. An occurrence with movable=true may instead be placed at its original start_local/venue or at any of its candidate_slots, subject to the after-hours rule above.
scheduling_rules.protected_rule: An occurrence with protected=true can never be moved and can only be left out of final_schedule if it directly conflicts with another scheduled occurrence whose vip_override=true; in every other situation a protected occurrence must appear in final_schedule at its original start_local/venue.
scheduling_rules.tie_break_rule: After the four objective_order metrics above are optimized, any remaining tie is broken by comparing, in ascending order of occurrence_id, the rank of each occurrence's chosen placement: rank 0 is its original start_local/venue, and ranks 1, 2, ... are its remaining candidate_slots entries in listed order, skipping any candidate_slots entry that exactly duplicates the original placement or an earlier candidate_slots entry (such a duplicate is not a distinct placement and receives no rank of its own). An occurrence left out of final_schedule entirely ranks after all of its distinct placements. The valid final_schedule is the one whose sequence of ranks, read in occurrence_id order, is lexicographically smallest.
scheduling_rules.vip_override_rule: An occurrence with vip_override=true is exempt from the after-hours cutoff. It is also the only thing that can unlock leaving a protected occurrence out of final_schedule (see protected_rule): if a scheduled vip_override occurrence directly conflicts with a protected occurrence, deferring that protected occurrence becomes a legal choice, and objective_order then decides -- like any other choice -- whether deferring it actually is optimal. Being vip_override=true does not by itself guarantee an occurrence beats a protected occurrence; it only makes that outcome reachable.
